evals.prompt: I need to document workflow_core/src/executor.rs for docs.rs. Add comprehensive doc comments explaining the executor trait and factory pattern.
evals.expected_output: Module-level docs explaining the executor abstraction, trait docs with why/when, factory pattern explanation, examples for implementing custom executors

assertions.name: executor_trait_documented
assertions.description: Executor trait has /// doc comment explaining why it exists and when to implement it

assertions.name: factory_trait_documented
assertions.description: ExecutorFactory trait has /// doc comment explaining the factory pattern

assertions.name: includes_implementation_example
assertions.description: At least one example showing how to implement a custom executor

assertions.name: cross_links_related_types
assertions.description: Uses [TypeName] syntax to link related types (e.g., [JobHandle], [JobStatus])

assertions.name: compiles_with_cargo_doc
assertions.description: cargo doc runs without warnings about broken links or malformed syntax

evals.prompt: can you add rustdoc to workflow_core/src/state.rs? it's the persistence layer and needs good docs before we publish
evals.expected_output: Module docs explaining state persistence, StateDb docs with SQLite details, TaskRecord docs with field explanations, examples showing resume workflow

assertions.name: has_module_level_docs
assertions.description: File starts with //! explaining the persistence layer

assertions.name: statedb_documented
assertions.description: StateDb struct has comprehensive docs explaining SQLite usage and resume capability

assertions.name: taskrecord_fields_documented
assertions.description: TaskRecord fields have doc comments explaining what they store

assertions.name: includes_resume_example
assertions.description: Example showing how state persistence enables workflow resume

assertions.name: documents_error_conditions
assertions.description: Methods document when they return Err and why

evals.prompt: document workflow_core/src/pipeline.rs - make sure to explain the DAG construction and why we validate for cycles
evals.expected_output: Module docs on DAG pipeline, Pipeline struct docs explaining construction and validation, method docs with invariants, example showing task dependency setup

assertions.name: has_module_level_docs
assertions.description: File starts with //! explaining the DAG pipeline concept

assertions.name: pipeline_struct_documented
assertions.description: Pipeline struct has docs explaining DAG construction and validation

assertions.name: explains_cycle_detection
assertions.description: Documentation explicitly explains why cycle detection is important

assertions.name: from_tasks_documented
assertions.description: from_tasks method has docs explaining validation and error conditions

assertions.name: includes_dependency_example
assertions.description: Example showing how to set up task dependencies

assertions.name: documents_invariants
assertions.description: Mentions invariants like 'tasks must have unique IDs' or 'no cycles allowed'
